beginner.question: What does flex-basis do in Flexbox?
beginner.answer: flex-basis sets the initial main-axis size of a flex item before flex-grow and flex-shrink are applied.

beginner.question: What is the default value of flex-basis?
beginner.answer: The default value of flex-basis is auto.

beginner.question: What does flex-basis: auto mean?
beginner.answer: flex-basis: auto looks at the item's width or height property or defaults to content size.

beginner.question: What does flex-basis: 0 mean?
beginner.answer: flex-basis: 0 sets the starting size to 0, so all space is distributed by flex-grow proportionally.

beginner.question: Can flex-basis use percentage values?
beginner.answer: Yes, flex-basis can be a percentage, relative to the parent container's main axis size.

beginner.question: How does flex-basis differ from width?
beginner.answer: flex-basis overrides width on the main axis when set to a non-auto value; width applies to the cross axis.

beginner.question: What does flex-basis: 200px do?
beginner.answer: flex-basis: 200px sets the item's initial main-axis size to 200px before any growing or shrinking.

beginner.question: How does flex-basis interact with flex-grow?
beginner.answer: flex-grow adds extra space on top of flex-basis to determine the final item size.

beginner.question: How does flex-basis interact with flex-shrink?
beginner.answer: flex-shrink reduces the item's size from its flex-basis when the container has insufficient space.

beginner.question: What happens when flex-basis is set on a column-direction container?
beginner.answer: In column direction, flex-basis controls the initial height instead of width.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between flex-basis: auto and flex-basis: content?
intermediate.answer: auto checks width/height property first, then content; content always sizes to the content's intrinsic size.

intermediate.question: How does flex-basis interact with min-width and max-width?
intermediate.answer: min-width and max-width clamp the final size; flex-basis is just the starting point before grow/shrink.

intermediate.question: How does flex-basis: 0 differ from flex-basis: 0px?
intermediate.answer: They are functionally the same; flex-basis accepts length values, and 0 is interpreted as 0px.

intermediate.question: How does flex-basis affect items with intrinsic aspect ratio?
intermediate.answer: flex-basis sets the main-axis size; the cross-axis size is adjusted to maintain the aspect ratio if no explicit cross-axis size is set.

intermediate.question: How does flex-basis work with replaced elements like images?
intermediate.answer: For replaced elements, flex-basis: auto uses the element's intrinsic size (the image's natural width).

intermediate.question: What is the difference between setting width: 50% and flex-basis: 50%?
intermediate.answer: In row direction, width: 50% sets cross-axis width; flex-basis: 50% sets initial main-axis width before grow/shrink.

intermediate.question: How does flex-basis: auto interact with box-sizing?
intermediate.answer: flex-basis: auto with box-sizing: border-box includes padding and border in the basis calculation.

intermediate.question: How does flex-basis behave in a multi-line flex container?
intermediate.answer: flex-basis sets the item's starting size on the main axis within each flex line; it does not affect line distribution.

intermediate.question: How do you use flex-basis to create flexible grids?
intermediate.answer: Set flex-basis: 33.333% on three items to create equal thirds, with flex-grow: 0 to prevent expansion.

intermediate.question: What happens to flex-basis when display: flex is applied to an inline element?
intermediate.answer: The inline element becomes blockified, and flex-basis applies as normal along the main axis.

advanced.question: How does the flex-basis algorithm resolve auto when width/height is set?
advanced.answer: If width is set (in row direction) and flex-basis is auto, the flex-basis equals the computed width value.

advanced.question: How does flex-basis interact with the writing-mode property?
advanced.answer: In vertical writing modes, flex-basis follows the block direction, so it controls the inline size rather than block size.

advanced.question: How does the auto value resolve when the item has only min-content size?
advanced.answer: If no width/height is set and content has no intrinsic size, auto resolves to min-content (shrink-to-fit).

advanced.question: How does flex-basis interact with max-content and min-content sizing keywords?
advanced.answer: flex-basis accepts max-content and min-content values, sizing the item to its maximum or minimum content size.

advanced.question: How does flex-basis affect the flex shorthands like flex: 1?
advanced.answer: flex: 1 sets flex-basis: 0, meaning the item starts at 0 and grows proportionally to fill the container.

advanced.question: How does flex-basis interact with the aspect-ratio property on flex items?
advanced.answer: When both are set, flex-basis determines the main axis size, and aspect-ratio determines the cross-axis size.

advanced.question: How does flex-basis work with the fit-content function?
advanced.answer: flex-basis: fit-content(300px) uses the content size but clamped to 300px maximum.

advanced.question: How does flex-basis behave with absolutely positioned flex children?
advanced.answer: Absolutely positioned children are not flex items, so flex-basis does not apply to them.

advanced.question: How does the CSS specification define the resolution of flex-basis for different display types?
advanced.answer: Flex-basis resolves based on the item's main-axis size; for blockified flex items, it uses the block-level width/height depending on direction.

advanced.question: How does flex-basis interact with the gap property in the total calculation?
advanced.answer: Gap is subtracted from the container before flex-bases are summed; the remaining space is distributed based on flex-grow.
